Laura Anderson has said she is praying for 'safe skies' as she has managed to get a flight to Edinburgh out of Dubai amid the esclating conflict in the Middle East, while Sam Gowland made his fourth attempt to flee the city.   Over the weekend, Iran launched a series of retaliatory air strikes against the United Arab Emirates and other targets after US-Israeli military operations against Iran, with significant damage caused in influencer hotspot Dubai.    Former Love Island star Laura, 36, took to Instagram on Wednesday and revealed she had managed to get a flight out of the UAE along with her two-year-old daughter Bonnie. A group of MPs sparked fury today after they were seen dancing in Parliament amid the deepening crisis in the Middle East. Strictly Come Dancing stars Angela Rippon and Alex Kingston led more than 40 MPs in a 'cha-cha-cha' across Westminster's Portcullis House. Sir Lindsay Hoyle, the Speaker of the House of Commons, danced with Rippon in an event to promote the advantages of dance for the nation's health and wellbeing. But some parliamentarians criticised the timing of the event in the wake of the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, which have plunged the Middle East into chaos. Iran has continued to lash out with retaliatory missile and drone attacks across the region, sparking fears of a wider conflict.

Husband of Labour MP Joani Reid among three arrested over 'spying for China'... amid fresh calls for 'super-embassy' to be blocked

Detectives detained three men earlier today on suspicion of assisting Chinese intelligence services contrary to the National Security Act. Ms Reid's husband David Taylor, 39, was arrested at an address in London; police also arrested a 68-year-old man at an address in Powys, Wales and a 43-year-old man in Pontyclun, Wales. One of the other arrested men is the partner of a former Labour MP, according to reports. The UK government has lodged formal protests with the Chinese authorities about the alleged activities. The news prompted demands for a review of the decision to grant permission for Beijing's new 'super-embassy' in London.
  • 1 video

Across the clear blue waters of the Gulf lies the coast of Iran, barely 100 miles away, and even nearer from some parts of the UAE. Liam Neeson's son Daniel has revealed he's undergone surgery on a rare congenital heart defect as he shared a new health update with his followers on Wednesday. In a post on Instagram, the tequila founder, 29, shared that he'd been previously diagnosed with the Bicuspid Aortic Valve (BAV), which was a condition where the aortic valve has 'two leaflets instead of the normal three.' Daniel explained that he was told he could require surgery later in life and had checks every two years, but began to suffer from 'severe left ventricle dilation and aortic enlargement.'

Ms McNally, 32, was stabbed at her home in Silverwood Green, Lurgan, on the night of December 18,  2022 when she was 15 weeks' pregnant. McCullagh, 35, from Woodland Gardens, Lisburn, denies murder. Belfast Crown Court heard on Tuesday from Ms McNally's father Noel and mother Bernadette, and her brothers Declan and Brendan.   • Adopted from birth here, I have to agree. Growing up not knowing who I look like, not knowing any family history, just that my own mother didn't want me - it all hurt deeply. Something I am still working on now at nearly 42. If I was born to biological parents, via surrogacy, I would feel so wanted, so loved and that my parents done anything to have me here. It's very different. There are so many kids waiting for adoption, because so many people are careless at creating lives. It's an easy option for many, with no consequences as to how that human being will feel growing up.

Hanoi is a remarkable place to visit, as is Vietnam as a whole.

A working railway running through central Hanoi has become an unlikely tourist magnet - and a serious safety risk. Known as Train Street, the narrow stretch sees trains barrel past homes and packed cafés, just inches from camera-wielding visitors in the Vietnamese capital. Although the line dates back to 1902, its popularity surged in 2017 after viral social media posts drew global attention. Since then, businesses have flourished along the tracks, drawing crowds of content creators and and curious tourists who time their visits with train schedules.
  • 14 comments
  • 1 video
  • share How Hanoi's most popular street could end up killing you

Email

Well I guess thats what happens when you bulldoze their habitats to build new houses for the new arrivals.

Motorists have discovered their cars are being stuffed with nuts by squirrels - amidst a 'very abnormal' rise in engine problems. A garage in Petersfield, Hampshire, said its vehicles had 'lost power' as an incident saw mechanics find 200 acorns stashed into one air intake pipe. Employees at the workshop said the problem is 'not uncommon' - adding they have seen it 'many times'. Squirrels are thought be stashing the acorns in the car pipes to keep them hidden. Typically, rodents bury their nuts in the ground or tree hollows.
  • 38 comments
  • share Town is blighted by squirrels hiding acorns in car engines
